title: Parathelypteris formosa
taxonomy: Parathelypteris formosa is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. The genus Parathelypteris is a group of ferns that were previously classified within the genus Thelypteris, but are now recognized as a distinct genus. This fern is native to East Asia and is often found growing in shaded, humid environments. Parathelypteris formosa is classified as a vascular plant, with characteristics such as rhizomes, fronds, and sporangia. The species is further classified into various subcategories based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.

The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
